rss.title: Minimum age of 11 set for UK puberty blocker trial
rss.snippet: Gender-questioning children will have to be at least 11 years old to take part in a clinical trial of puberty-blocking drugs.

rss.title: Five tips to keep your kids cool this weekend
rss.snippet: Temperatures are set to rise over the next few days, and children can be especially vulnerable - so read on for tips to protect them.

rss.title: Young women now have 'close to zero' risk of cervical cancer death after HPV jab
rss.snippet: A new study finds that hundreds of lives have been saved since school-age girls were offered the HPV jab in 2008.

github_repos.snippet: ODK Collect is an Android app for filling out forms. It's been used to collect billions of data points in challenging environments around the world. Contribute and make the world a better place! ✨📋✨

arxiv_papers.title: VLMs May Not Globally Enhance Human Alignment over LLMs During Natural Reading
arxiv_papers.snippet: Large language models (LLMs) have become increasingly useful computational models of human language processing, but it remains unclear whether vision-language learning makes text representations more human-like during natural reading. arxiv_papers.title: Global stability of a Hebbian/anti-Hebbian network for principal subspace learning
arxiv_papers.snippet: Biological neural networks self-organize according to local synaptic modifications to produce stable computations. youtube_items.summary: 2025 [music] was a year of major achievements and challenges for global health and for who. The most significant [music] achievement was the adoption of the WHO pandemic agreement. A powerful example of what multilateralism can deliver when countries choose cooperation over division. In addition, this year WHO validated [music] 13 countries for the elimination of diseases. We provided guidance [music] on innovations including Lenapavir for HIV prevention and GLP-1 [music] therapies for obesity and we responded to 48 emergencies in 79 [music] countries and territories including Gaza, Sudan and Ukraine. 2025 [music] brought other serious challenges. youtube_items.title: [YT] YouTube:j7ysRCMNWlk
youtube_items.summary: In the year 2025, the issue of global inequality remains a daunting challenge for humanity. Today, we explore the ever widening gap between rich and poor, a divide that continues to shape our world socially, economically, and politically. Despite technological advancements and economic growth, the chasm seems wider than ever, sparking debates and concerns across various platforms. The disparity between wealth and poverty isn't just a statistic. It's a reality impacting billions of lives. While a small percentage of the global population enjoys unprecedented riches, a staggering number of people struggle to meet basic needs. In 2025, it's estimated that the top 1% of the world's richest individuals control nearly half of the global wealth, whereas billions are barely surviving on a few doll

youtube_items.title: [YT] YouTube:3jBwZ7vDYUM
youtube_items.summary: Hello everyone. My name is Kyla Parks and today I'll be presenting on global health inequality. This issue explores how and why health outcomes differ dramatically across countries and populations and why it matters to all of us. Global health inequality refers to unfair and avoidable differences in health status and access to healthc care resources across population populations. These gaps are shaped by income, geography, gender, and social or political systems. At its core, it's about who has the chance to live a long and healthy life and who does not. To give you a sense of scale, in highincome countries, average life expectancy is about 80 years. In some lowincome countries is closer to 60.
